summary: Write a beginner runbook for provisioning/running the pinned Minecraft (Paper) server locally and commit a committed start script.
implementation:
1. scripts/minecraft/start-server.sh: new bash script following the scripts/check-services.sh house style (`#!/usr/bin/env bash`, `set -euo pipefail`, `✓/✗` echo helpers). Behavior: (a) verify Java is on PATH and meets the major version pinned by E1-R1 (docs/decisions/0001-minecraft-version-and-server.md), printing the install hint and exiting non-zero if absent; (b) resolve config from env with documented defaults so it binds to the E1 decisions rather than hardcoding: SERVER_DIR (default ./minecraft-server), MC_VERSION and PAPER_BUILD (default values = the version/build pinned in 0001; comment cites the decision doc as source of truth), MEM (default 2G), ONLINE_MODE (default false per E1-R2 / docs/decisions/0002-auth-mode.md); (c) create SERVER_DIR; (d) download the Paper jar from the Paper API only if not already present (idempotent), into SERVER_DIR; (e) write eula.txt with eula=true and echo the plain-language EULA acknowledgement; (f) if server.properties is absent, generate a minimal one (online-mode from ONLINE_MODE, motd, max-players, difficulty, view-distance, white-list=true with a note; leave level-seed/level-type defaults — world config is deferred to E2-2); (g) launch the jar with `java -Xms${MEM} -Xmx${MEM} -jar <jar> nogui` from SERVER_DIR; (h) add an optional `--smoke` flag that boots, tails logs until the `Done (` ready line (with a timeout) then sends `stop`, used for verification. Make it executable (chmod +x).
2. docs/minecraft/server-setup.md: new beginner runbook. Sections: what this gets you and what is out of scope (link E2-3 cloud hosting, E2-4 supervision); prerequisites; install Java per OS (macOS/Homebrew, Debian/Ubuntu, Windows/WSL) matching the major version pinned in 0001; what Paper is and why we use it (1-paragraph plain-language summary, link to docs/decisions/0001-minecraft-version-and-server.md); the EULA — what accepting it means and where eula.txt lives; run the committed start script (copy-paste block, env-var override table mirroring the script defaults); first boot — what to expect in the console and the `Done (` ready line; server.properties essentials explained in plain language (online-mode and the offline-mode security/EULA tradeoff cross-linking docs/decisions/0002-auth-mode.md, difficulty, motd, max-players, white-list, view-distance; note world seed/type is configured later in E2-2); stop the server (`stop` in console or Ctrl+C); verify success by connecting a vanilla client to localhost:25565; troubleshooting (wrong Java version, port 25565 in use, jar download failed, EULA not accepted). 3. Note in the runbook that defaults track the E1 decision docs; if docs/decisions/0001/0002 are not yet present, the script defaults still boot a working server and the doc flags them as the authoritative source once merged.

testing.reason: Deliverables are a documentation runbook plus an idempotent ops/provisioning shell script. No application modules or runtime code paths change, and the repo has no bash unit-test harness (scripts/*.sh such as check-services.sh ship without tests). Correctness is established via shellcheck + bash -n + the --dry-run script verification below, plus a committed pytest module (tests/backend/test_minecraft_start_server.py) exercised by the standard `pnpm test` runner.

verification.command: shellcheck scripts/minecraft/start-server.sh && bash -n scripts/minecraft/start-server.sh && SERVER_DIR="$(mktemp -d)" bash scripts/minecraft/start-server.sh --dry-run
verification.instructions: Deterministic, Node-free / Java-free / network-free. method MUST be "script" (a shell command run via runScriptVerify with an exit-code check) — it is NOT "boot" (which would run `node -e "import('<command>')"` and crash with ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND because this is a shell command, not a JS module entry point; that mis-mapping was the prior verification failure). The command lint-checks (shellcheck) and syntax-checks (bash -n) the start script, then runs it in --dry-run against a throwaway SERVER_DIR: --dry-run exercises the full provisioning logic — Java probe (warn-only in dry-run, no JRE required), EULA acceptance (writes eula.txt), minimal server.properties generation with the pinned E1-R1/E1-R2 defaults, and the never-clobber guard — without downloading the Paper jar or launching the JVM. Exit 0 proves a fresh directory is correctly provisioned. The real end-to-end boot is exercised on a Java-21 host via `scripts/minecraft/start-server.sh --smoke`; E2-1 has no LLM runtime path (pure server provisioning) so no LM Studio validation applies, and this --dry-run path is the nearest realistic local verification.
verification.reason: Acceptance requires a fresh machine provision a correct server by following only the committed doc/script. The verifier sandbox has no Java 21 and no network, so the deterministic proof is lint + syntax + a --dry-run that generates the EULA and the E1-pinned server.properties from a clean directory; the Java-dependent end-to-end boot is documented as `--smoke` for Java-21 hosts.
